response.justification: Bogdan brings valuable geospatial domain expertise and a demonstrated commitment to distributed-systems thinking, making him culturally aligned with the role's technical requirements. However, there is a critical language-skills gap: his modern C++ experience ended in 2014–2015, and he has no Rust background despite both being explicit requirements. For a mid-senior individual contributor role, immediate productivity in the primary tech stack is typically expected. His recent trajectory (TypeScript, Node.js, web services) is distant from systems-level programming. An interview could assess his ability and motivation to ramp up quickly on modern C++, but this gap presents material risk.
strengths: Direct geospatial domain expertise (GISCollective founder, Green Map GIS developer) relevant to routing-tile data pipelines
Proven distributed-systems experience handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby, plus Kubernetes and infrastructure knowledge
Strong software craftsmanship foundation with TDD, CI/CD, and quality-first practices consistently applied across roles
concerns: Critical language gap: last C++ work was 2014–2015 (11+ years ago); no Rust experience despite both being required or acceptable alternatives
Career trajectory shifted to application-level TypeScript/Node.js, away from systems programming needed for production routing pipelines
Geospatial experience is primarily open-source data collection and mapping, not production-scale navigation routing guidance systems

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain knowledge and prior GIS work
